Off Calendar Aug-24-2018 Off Calendar

No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY HARRIS' Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Fords Customer Relationship Center Representative Christopher Quinn.

Off calendar. Notice of settlement of entire case filed on August 13, 2018.

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Jul-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Larry Chae, Veracis Law Corporation, Telephone Numer(714)453-9015, Attorney for Plaintiff;
Charles F. Harlow, Schnaders Attorneys at Law, Telepone Number (415)364-6731, Attorney for Defendant Ford Motor Company.

Ruling: PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY HARRIS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One is denied in part and granted in part. Moving party is to prepare an order. Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein; Clerk: William Trupek; Not Reported, (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar May-18-2018 Continued To Jul-10-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

The Court stated that it had been notified by the parties that they were stipulating to the Court's tentative ruling: The Court intends to address the issues raised by this motion in a three-step process. The parties shall first agree or the Court will impose an appropriate Protective Order referable to documents to be produced. Discussion of the Protective Order shall be the subject of the hearing. The parties shall bring to the hearing their proposed versions of a Protective Order. After a Protective Order is in place the Defendant shall have twenty days to produce documents agreed to be produced. Within twenty days following receipt of the documents the parties shall meet and confer to determine document categories that remain open requiring Court adjudication, notifying the Court accordingly. A further hearing date for rulings on objections will be scheduled.

The Court further noted that the parties stated that they had agreed upon a protective order. The protective order is to be submitted to Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein for review and signature.

Ruling: Plaintiff Timothy Harris' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One is continued to a date "to be determined" for the Court to rule on the the merits raised in the motion.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (302/JPT)

***Per Mr. Stein, Following an Interim Order filed 5/16/18, the matter is continued to July 10, 2018 at 9am.**

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-26-2018 Set For Trial On Oct-09-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
